items.headline: China brokers talks between Pakistan and Afghanistan after border clashes
items.body: Pakistan and Afghanistan's Taliban government resumed peace talks in China's Urumqi after weeks of fighting that killed hundreds. Beijing is mediating between the two sides to broker a lasting ceasefire, with both countries welcoming China's efforts.

items.headline: Cuba pardons over 2,000 prisoners amid pressure from Washington
items.body: Cuba pardoned 2,010 prisoners as a humanitarian gesture during Holy Week, the second such release this year during talks with the Trump administration. Releasing political prisoners has long been a core US demand as Washington enforces an oil blockade on the island.

items.headline: Chinese carriers raise fuel surcharges as oil shock hits aviation
items.body: Air China, China Southern Airlines and subsidiaries announced fuel surcharges will rise by 60 yuan for flights under 800 kilometres and 120 yuan for longer routes starting April 5. Global jet fuel prices more than doubled to $197 per barrel in recent weeks, up from $95.50 a month earlier.

items.headline: Kenya reports 16 nationals missing in Russia after military recruitment
items.body: Kenya's foreign minister reported 16 Kenyans are missing in Russia after joining the country's army, with 38 others in Russian hospitals under restricted access. An estimated 165 Kenyans are actively involved in military operations, bringing the total who have participated in the conflict in Ukraine to over 250.
